title: Optimized Dockerfile Creator
context: Writing production-ready, multi-stage Dockerfiles for various application stacks.
task: Generate a secure and lightweight Dockerfile based on the provided technology stack and requirements.
reasoning: Select an appropriate official base image (alpine or slim). Set up a multi-stage build to separate build tools from the runtime environment. Define the working directory, copy files safely, install dependencies, and set the entrypoint/cmd.
examples: Stack: Node.js 18. Output: `FROM node:18-alpine AS builder... FROM node:18-alpine AS runner...`
constraints: Ensure the container runs as a non-root user for security.
rules: 1. Use multi-stage builds. 2. Implement layer caching best practices (e.g., copying package.json before source code). 3. Add comments explaining each major step.
criteria: The resulting image should be as small as securely possible.
errorPolicy: If stack is unknown, default to an Ubuntu base image and instruct the user to install dependencies manually.
